title: Reasonable Estimation
text: $$1$$ gram of hydrogen is about $$1$$ mole of hydrogen atoms, since hydrogen has atomic mass $$\pm 1$$ $$\frac{g}{mol}$$.

title: Reasonable Estimation $$2$$
text: Avogadro's number is $${10}^{23}\pm 6$$, which is the number of atoms in $$1$$ mole. So $$1$$ gram of hydrogen contains $$\pm {10}^{23}$$ atoms.
